name: Iglesias Loans, LLC
description_short: Iglesias Loans specializes in commercial real estate financing for churches and nonprofit organizations, offering purchase, construction, refinance, and consolidation loans with expertise in nonprofit financial qualification.
description_long:
Iglesias Loans was founded by Carlos Iglesias and has spent the past decade focused exclusively on serving churches and nonprofit organizations seeking commercial real estate financing. The company positions itself as a specialized lender that understands the unique financial structures and challenges nonprofit entities face when attempting to secure traditional bank loans.

Iglesias Loans offers four primary lending products: commercial real estate purchases, construction and improvement loans, refinancing of existing loans, and loan consolidation. The company works with clients from initial consultation through loan closing, helping organizations prepare financial documentation and understand lender requirements. They also partner with commercial real estate agents to assist with property selection and transactions. Their service model involves a three-step process: discussing the organization's project needs, reviewing church financials and creating qualification plans, and identifying appropriate loan options from available lenders.

What distinguishes Iglesias Loans is their stated specialization in nonprofit loan structures and requirements. They explicitly acknowledge that "not every lender understands non-profit finances" and position their value as helping churches and nonprofits prepare proper documentation and financial presentations for traditional lenders. The company emphasizes hands-on support throughout the lending process and claims to have financial programs available for organizations not currently loan-ready.

The company operates as a loan broker or loan originator rather than a direct lender, facilitating connections between nonprofit borrowers and traditional commercial lenders. Client testimonials indicate satisfaction with their expertise and communication. However, the website provides limited information about loan terms, interest rates, processing times, or specific lenders they work with. No pricing transparency is disclosed, and the company appears to market primarily to faith-based and mission-driven organizations rather than general commercial real estate borrowers.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Specializes exclusively in nonprofit and church lending, understanding unique financial qualification challenges
Offers hands-on support from application through closing, not just transactional involvement
Has commercial real estate agent partnerships to assist with property selection and negotiation
Provides financial preparation programs for organizations not yet ready to qualify for loans
Bilingual services (English/Spanish) serving diverse church communities
Ten years of stated experience in church and nonprofit financing
Transparent about bank requirements and helps organizations understand what lenders expect
cons: No pricing information disclosed — rates, fees, and APRs not listed on website
Functions as a broker rather than direct lender, so final terms depend on third-party lenders
Limited information about processing timelines or approval rates
Narrow specialization means they may not serve general commercial real estate borrowers
No information provided about minimum loan amounts or eligibility requirements
best_for: Churches seeking to purchase property, expand facilities, or refinance existing loans
Nonprofit organizations struggling to qualify for commercial real estate loans through traditional banks
Faith-based organizations needing guidance on nonprofit financial documentation for lenders
Spanish-speaking churches and nonprofits in the Texas area (based on phone number)
diagnosis: Iglesias Loans is best for churches and nonprofit organizations seeking commercial real estate financing who need specialized guidance on nonprofit financial qualification and documentation. The main caveat is that they operate as brokers connecting borrowers to third-party lenders rather than funding loans directly, so final terms and availability depend on external lender decisions, and no pricing information is transparently disclosed upfront.
services: Commercial real estate purchase loans for churches and nonprofits
Loan refinancing for existing church and nonprofit debt
Church financial qualification assessment and planning
Commercial real estate agent partnerships and referrals
Financial readiness coaching for organizations not yet qualified
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 8245 North Fwy Building A, Houston, TX 77037
